statusDefinitions.YES: Local evidence supports the capability through executable behavior, tests, contracts, drift checks, audit artifacts, or a scoped exception.
statusDefinitions.PARTIAL: Local evidence exists, but required behavior, coverage, enforcement, or selected-profile proof is incomplete.
statusDefinitions.NO: The capability is not implemented in this repo.
statusDefinitions.UNAVAILABLE: The capability cannot currently run in this repo or surface; an unavailable reason and recovery path are required.
statusDefinitions.UNKNOWN: The capability has not yet been assessed against local evidence.

capabilities.title: Historical incident traceability

verification.summary: Committed incident trace documents exist, but this packet forbids docs-only or generated-audit-only proof from supporting YES.

blockers.description: The row currently depends on committed prose and generated closeout audit paths rather than committed executable, test, contract, or scoped-exception proof.
blockers.recoveryCommand: Add a committed contract or drift check that validates incident ids referenced by the trace artifacts.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Add a committed incident-trace validator that checks required incident ids and source links.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: The trace artifacts are useful and committed, but they are prose evidence; this packet requires stronger proof before YES.

capabilities.title: Requirements trace to original closeout rules

verification.summary: Committed requirements trace documents exist, but this packet forbids docs-only or generated-audit-only proof from supporting YES.

blockers.description: The row currently depends on committed prose and generated closeout audit paths rather than committed executable, test, contract, or scoped-exception proof.
blockers.recoveryCommand: Add a committed trace validator that checks requirement ids, row links, and profile mappings against the ledger.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Add a committed requirement-trace validator and wire it into the closeout test or drift-check suite.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: The trace remains valuable as source material, but this packet treats it as prose until a committed validator exists.

capabilities.title: Machine-readable capability ledger schema

verification.summary: The schema defines strict status, evidence, blocker, and YES guardrails; this ledger is validated against it.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Keep schema validation in the closeout validation path as ledger rows evolve.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: YES depends on local schema validation, not on the schema text alone.

capabilities.title: Capability ledger populated with frozen row inventory

verification.summary: The committed ledger contains the frozen row inventory and a committed regression test verifies row presence, uniqueness, status summary parity, and YES proof guardrails.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Keep FROZEN_CLOSEOUT_CAPABILITY_ROWS synchronized with any future shared row inventory change.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: YES is supported by committed ledger content plus the committed row-inventory regression test.

capabilities.title: Structured adjudication protocol for ambiguity

verification.summary: Ambiguous and high-impact closeout paths use candidate/report/tuple evidence and block or retain when review evidence is missing or stale.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Continue adding scenario-specific packets as new ambiguous closeout scenarios are introduced.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Covered by review/quorum, stale tuple, retained candidate, and agent-remediation tests.

capabilities.title: Declared review surfaces

verification.summary: Review sources are policy-controlled and tests verify trusted review source and quorum behavior.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Add a policy entry before accepting any new review surface.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Allowed review sources and quorum rules are config-driven.

capabilities.title: Candidate evidence packet

verification.summary: Repo sweep, agent conflict, dirty split, and prune paths write structured candidate evidence before high-impact action.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Keep packet fields synchronized with any new symbolic action.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Tests cover candidate ids, investigation packets, conflict packets, and queue packets.

capabilities.title: Adjudication report

verification.summary: Review/quorum and retained-candidate paths require durable reports or explicit blockers before mutation or retain.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Extend report validation when new reviewer outcomes are added.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Review artifacts are event-recorded and fake or stale reports are rejected by tests.

capabilities.title: Adjudication to symbolic action boundary

verification.summary: Reviewer output is treated as approval of symbolic action only; repo-owned actors revalidate and mutate.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Require every new symbolic action to enter the policy catalog and contract tests.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Tests cover readonly reviewer enforcement and config-driven symbolic action approval.

capabilities.title: Repo-owned mutation after adjudication

verification.summary: Mutation runs through repo-owned Python actors and PowerShell adapters after policy, tuple, dirty-state, and validation checks.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Keep all mutating adapters routed through the bounded closeout CLI.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Closeout actions are cataloged in policy and implementation symbol tests.

capabilities.title: Exact mutation tuple and stale tuple rejection

verification.summary: Tuple hashes include candidate/action/evidence/policy/pins and stale tuples block mutation across finalize, sweep, dirty split, and agent queue paths.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Expand tuple coverage if new high-impact symbolic actions are introduced.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Tests include stale review tuple, dirty split stale tuple, and agent result tuple mismatch cases.

capabilities.title: Bounded wrapper authority

verification.summary: Closeout subprocesses run through a bounded runner with timeout, output cap, failure-text normalization, child-process cleanup, and audit evidence.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Keep any new closeout PowerShell adapter behind Invoke-CloseoutCli.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Tests cover hung children, output caps, known failure text with zero exit, finalizer semantic success overriding validation vocabulary, and orphan cleanup.

capabilities.title: Broker manifest and dirty baseline

verification.summary: Work blocks capture manifest identity, start head, path claims, lease, and dirty baseline before repair or finalize.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Preserve baseline capture in any new session bootstrap or bridge hook.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Current ledger work block captured an empty dirty baseline for the claimed ledger path.

capabilities.title: Deterministic work-block selection

verification.summary: Finalize selects an explicit or uniquely active work block and blocks ambiguous or stale selection state.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Keep selection-reason output mandatory when finalize runs without explicit workBlockId.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Protected target no-op avoids synthetic work block creation on clean protected target.

capabilities.title: Dirty-state classification before mutation

verification.summary: Detection classifies owned, foreign, mixed, unowned, and generated/exempt dirty state before repair, finalize, sweep, or final gate.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Add new dirty classes to tests before policy relies on them.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Tests cover current worktree dirty ownership classification and generated closeout packet classification.

capabilities.title: Foreign dirty preservation

verification.summary: Foreign dirty paths are retained, audited, and excluded from unrelated staging, pruning, and independent closeout.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Continue requiring exact target-overlap proof before any foreign-dirty branch/worktree remediation.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Tests cover independent closeout with retained foreign dirty, safe local pruning, and foreign dirty target-overlap blockers.

capabilities.title: Baseline-dirty mixed path protection

verification.summary: Paths dirty at broker baseline are not whole-file checkpointed as current work; overlaps block or enter split/remediation paths.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: If hunk-level ownership is added, keep whole-file baseline-dirty protection as the default fallback.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Tests include clean-at-start checkpointing, baseline dirty overlap blocker, and exact owned-path staging.

capabilities.title: Hard-clean final gate

verification.summary: Final completion is blocked unless dirty state, branch state, worktrees, stashes, queue state, generated artifacts, and blockers are clean or accounted for.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Keep hard-clean checks synchronized with any new generated/exempt or queue roots.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Tests cover non-exempt dirty files, protected target dirty state, retained remote refs, and pending agent queue blockers.

capabilities.title: repo_closed_for_final_response postcondition

verification.summary: The repo-owned finalizer reports repo_closed_for_final_response only after the hard-clean postcondition succeeds.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Keep pre-final bridge checks subordinate to this repo-owned postcondition.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: The previous implementation-prompt work block closed clean with this postcondition at head 118bc1cb.

capabilities.title: Advisory hooks are non-authoritative

verification.summary: Bridge and response/final hooks write reminders/status and feed repo gates without becoming mutation authority.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Keep hook additions read-only unless they are promoted to tested repo-owned actors.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Tooling baseline requires bridge hooks to pass SkipSessionWorktree and remediation-freeze guard checks.

capabilities.title: Response hooks do not resurrect managed worktrees

verification.summary: Pre-response bootstrap records lightweight broker state without creating or refreshing managed session worktrees.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Keep SkipSessionWorktree present in all bridge reminder paths.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Tests include pre-response broker bootstrap dirty-baseline recording without worktree lifecycle mutation.

capabilities.title: Final utilities write before clean gate or to generated/exempt paths

verification.summary: Generated closeout artifacts, evidence repair, metrics, handoffs, and conflict packets are declared generated/exempt or run before the hard-clean gate.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Register any new final utility output in generated/exempt policy and contract tests.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Tests cover generated closeout conflict packets and evidence repair path ownership.

capabilities.title: Tooling drift detection

verification.summary: Policy, required symbols, adapters, config keys, test names, and generated path contracts are checked so stale tooling is non-authoritative.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Add baseline symbols whenever closeout behavior becomes mandatory policy.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Tests include tooling stale without bounded runner and policy verifier drift checks.

capabilities.title: Repo sweep read-only planning

verification.summary: Repo hygiene and closeout sweep planning may inspect many candidates and emit structured plans before mutation.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Keep planning read-only even as new candidate kinds are added.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Tests cover structured scan artifacts, candidate ids, manual-only source-like directories, and repo sweep candidate reports.

capabilities.title: Repo sweep single-candidate mutation default

verification.summary: Repo sweep apply now mutates a single explicit candidate by default, blocks multi-candidate broad apply, and requires an audited bulk override for exact multi-candidate mutation.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Keep every new mutating repo sweep candidate kind represented in applyScope and the broad-apply blocker test.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Regression tests cover candidate-id-only mutation, multi-candidate broad apply blocking, and audited exact bulk override.

capabilities.title: Audited bulk mutation override

verification.summary: Repo sweep bulk mutation is disabled by default and only proceeds when policy enables it and the supplied override exactly matches the planned candidate ids and per-candidate tuples.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Keep override tuple fields synchronized with any new repo sweep symbolic action.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: The CLI and PowerShell wrapper accept an override file, but repo-owned validation remains the authority for permission, reviewer approval, candidate set, tuple match, audit reason, and recovery command.

capabilities.title: No force-push target recovery

verification.summary: Target non-fast-forward recovery fetches and re-pins or reports rerun; automatic target force-push is not used.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Keep force-push absent from all automated target race recovery paths.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Test coverage includes non-fast-forward fetch/update/rerun behavior.

capabilities.title: Protected target no-op closeout

verification.summary: A clean protected target can close as a no-op only after repo-closed verification; dirty protected target blocks.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Keep dirty/stash/worktree blockers active for protected target no-op closeout.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: The previous work block used protected target no-op as part of clean final closeout.

capabilities.title: Target push race recovery

verification.summary: The repo fetches and reports rerun/update evidence for target races, but full bounded rebuild/retry automation remains incomplete.

blockers.description: Current behavior covers fetch/re-pin/rerun blocker semantics, but Standard full retry/rebuild automation is not fully proven.
blockers.recoveryCommand: Implement bounded rebuild/retry after fetched target movement, add tests for safe retry and terminal blocker, then rerun ledger validation.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Add a safe retry path test after target fetch/re-pin when validation still passes.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Core no-force-push invariant is YES; this Standard retry convenience remains PARTIAL.

capabilities.title: Retained-candidate remediation actor

verification.summary: The retained-candidate remediation actor wraps repo sweep and applies exactly one promoted candidate per run.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Keep candidate-specific remediation packets required for merge failures.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Tests include retained actor applies one candidate per run and merge-failed report has agent-resolution packet.

capabilities.title: Unavailable or insufficient review surface blocker

verification.summary: Agent-queue unavailable packets, review-quorum insufficient blockers, and every declared review surface now have durable unavailable report proof.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Keep declared review surfaces synchronized with reviewQuorum.declaredSurfaces and require unavailable reports for any new surface.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: The table-driven regression test writes review_surface_unavailable reports for every declared surface; check_review_quorum exposes insufficient_review_quorum and declared surface details.

capabilities.title: Checked-out and locked worktree handling

verification.summary: Repo sweep distinguishes clean stale locked worktrees, protected locked worktrees, dirty detached worktrees, and exact-policy cleanup.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Keep protected locked worktree cleanup exact-policy only.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Tests include stale locked cleanup, protected exact-policy cleanup, protected inspect-only, and dirty detached preservation blockers.

capabilities.title: Independent review quorum

verification.summary: High-impact actions require a self reviewer plus two independent 10/10 reviewers; insufficient or undeclared review surfaces fail closed.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Keep reviewer identities and required scores in reviewQuorum and autoQuorum covered by tests when policy changes.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Validated by test_review_quorum_requires_allowed_ten_score_self_plus_two_independent and auto-quorum exact tuple tests.

capabilities.title: Git hook gates

verification.summary: The repo has a hook-guard CLI and remediation-freeze hook tests, but no committed hook installer or tracked Git hook adapter proves local pre-commit/pre-push enforcement.

blockers.description: Hook-guard behavior is tested, but actual Git hooks live under .git/hooks and are not committed local proof.
blockers.recoveryCommand: Add a committed hook installer or tracked hook template plus a validation test proving pre-commit and pre-push route through hook-guard.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Add tracked hook templates or an installer and test that installed hooks call hook-guard through Invoke-CloseoutCli.ps1.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: This does not block Core adoption, but it blocks Standard maturity for hook-level enforcement.

capabilities.title: Agent remediation queue

verification.summary: Merge-failed candidates can be promoted to scoped queue packets; result collection validates tuple, dirty hash, result scope, and blockers before returning symbolic next action.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Keep result collection symbolic until repo-owned finalize consumes validated results.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Tests cover queue policy, stale packets, dirty byte drift, result root scope, blockers, failed validation, missing fields, wrong shard identity, and repo-closed pending queue blockers.

capabilities.title: Automated subagent dispatch

verification.summary: Repo-owned planning can shard eligible queue packets for Codex background agents, but actual Desktop subagent spawning is surface-provided and not fully repo-provable.

blockers.description: The repo can plan one background agent per eligible shard and write unavailable packets, but spawning agents is a Codex Desktop surface behavior outside repo-owned mutation authority.
blockers.recoveryCommand: Exercise the Codex Desktop adapter on a live queued conflict, collect result packets, and update the row if the surface produces durable proof.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Run a live surface smoke test that spawns from queued conflict shards and collects scoped result packets.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Planning and unavailable behavior are tested; actual spawn transport remains a Surface Plugin concern.

capabilities.title: Evidence-preserving prune

verification.summary: Historical, non-ancestor, stale transaction, and dirty detached cleanup preserve recovery evidence before deletion and reject missing or stale recovery artifacts.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Keep recovery roots content-addressed and out-of-root artifacts blocking deletion.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Tests cover bundle-backed recovery, reviewer verdicts, hash checks, stale recovery rejection, and final sweep after prune.

capabilities.title: Closeout remediation freeze

verification.summary: Freeze marker and process env block lifecycle mutation, allow generated content-addressed audit packets, and require quorum/revalidation for removal.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Keep lifecycle hook guard calls in every new hook/publish/finalize path.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Tests cover lifecycle blocking, process-scoped env, audit packets, triage, removal quorum, and hard-clean freeze blockers.

capabilities.title: Dirty split automation

verification.summary: Owned dirty clusters can be preserved on broker-owned branches/worktrees with exact tuple checks while foreign dirty is retained.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Keep split operations one candidate per run and stale-tuple guarded.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Tests cover auto-remediation, stale tuple rejection, sparse worktree reuse, one-candidate mutation, repair ordering, and foreign dirty retention.

capabilities.title: Runtime service lifecycle around closeout

verification.summary: Configured runtime services can stop before validation and restart only after promoted target validation and repo-closed success.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Enable concrete service entries only when a repo-owned runtime service executes repo code.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Runtime lifecycle support is installed and tested; current service entries may be disabled unless a preview/runtime service is actively configured.

capabilities.title: Remote feature clean integration and prune

verification.summary: Remote feature refs are compared against target, clean-integrated or pruned when integrated/patch-equivalent, and retained when unique or conflicting.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Keep remote deletion ordered after target update success and recovery/audit proof.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Tests cover integrated, patch-equivalent, unique, and conflicting remote feature branches.

capabilities.title: Retained candidate auto-closeout remediation bundle

verification.summary: The retained-candidate remediation actor can auto-decide exact tuples, preserve before prune, clean-integrate or prune policy-eligible refs, and fail closed with durable blockers for ambiguity.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Keep bundle coverage tied to retained remediation, remote feature, prune recovery, and repo-closed tests as policy changes.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Bundle classifications: auto-decide-with-proof, auto-preserve-then-prune, auto-merge-then-prune, and fail-closed-with-durable-blocker. Manual disposition remains expected only for policy-excluded or data-loss-ambiguous candidates.

capabilities.title: Local-only repository closeout

verification.summary: Repos without remotes can still update local target, prune the feature branch, and pass closeout safety checks without remote assumptions.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Keep local-only path covered when target/ref handling changes.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: Test coverage includes no-origin local-only closeout updating target and pruning the branch.

capabilities.title: Clean standard non-smuggling rule

verification.summary: The standard and profiles split Core, Standard, Max, Conditional, Surface Plugin, Reference Only, and Non-Goal rows, and this ledger records optional rows honestly.

capabilities.smallestNextStep: Keep non-Core rows marked PARTIAL, NO, UNAVAILABLE, UNKNOWN, or optional unless local evidence proves selected-profile support.
capabilities.repoSpecificNotes: This row is backed by the ledger's actual non-YES rows and closeout audit, not just the standard text.

notes: This ledger intentionally leaves historical traceability, requirements trace, target race full retry, git hook gates, and automated subagent dispatch as PARTIAL rather than documentation-only, incomplete, local-hook-only, or surface-only YES.
Generated .claude-state audit paths are cited as local evidence roots; they remain generated/exempt and are not source work, and do not alone support YES.
